 {"id":"beads_rust-l7hz","title":"EPIC: Installation & Distribution Automation","description":"# Installation & Distribution Automation

## Background & Rationale

Based on research of mature Rust/Go CLI tools (xf, cass, beads_viewer) and 2025-2026 distribution best practices, br needs a professional-grade installation and update system that makes adoption frictionless and secure.

### Why This Matters
- One-liner installation is the gold standard for CLI tool adoption
- Users expect tools to auto-update (or easily update) themselves
- Multi-platform support (Linux, macOS, Windows; x86_64, ARM64) is table stakes
- Security requires checksum verification and signed releases
- AI coding agents benefit from automated tool management

## Goals
Deliver a complete distribution system that enables users to install br with a single command, receive automatic updates, and trust the integrity of downloaded binaries.

## Deliverables

### 1. Multi-Platform Installer Script
- Platform detection (Linux/macOS/Windows, amd64/arm64)
- Download from GitHub Releases with checksum verification
- Fallback to source build if binary unavailable
- Idempotent installation (safe to re-run)
- Lock mechanism to prevent concurrent installs
- Resume capability for interrupted downloads
- PATH modification with shell detection

### 2. Self-Update Command
- `br update` - Check for and install updates
- `br update --check` - Check only, don't install
- `br update --force` - Force reinstall current version
- Use `self_update` or `patchify` crate
- Ed25519 signature verification
- SHA256 hash verification of downloaded files
- Streaming downloads for large files

### 3. Release Automation
- GitHub Actions workflow for multi-platform builds
- Automatic checksum generation
- Release asset naming convention
- Changelog generation from closed issues

### 4. Package Manager Distribution
- Homebrew tap: `brew install dicklesworthstone/tap/br`
- Scoop bucket for Windows users
- AUR package for Arch Linux
- crates.io publishing for Rust users

### 5. Version Management
- Semantic versioning (SemVer)
- `br version` shows build info, git commit, build date
- `br version --check` shows if update available

## Acceptance Criteria
- Single-command installation on all supported platforms
- `br update` successfully updates the binary in-place
- All downloads verified with checksums
- Installation is fully idempotent
- Works behind corporate proxies (HTTPS_PROXY support)

## Technical Approach

### Installer Script Pattern
```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/.../install.sh | bash
# OR
curl -fsSL https://br.tools/install | bash
```

### Self-Update with self_update crate
```toml
[dependencies]
self_update = { version = "0.27", features = ["rustls"], default-features = false }
```

### Release Profile (already in place)
```toml
[profile.release]
opt-level = "z"     # Size optimization
lto = true           # Link-time optimization
codegen-units = 1    # Single codegen unit
panic = "abort"      # No unwinding
strip = true         # Remove symbols
```

## Security Considerations
- Ed25519 signatures for release files
- SHA256 checksums in separate .sha256 files
- HTTPS-only downloads (rustls, no OpenSSL)
- Verify before replace (atomic update)

## References
- self_update crate: https://github.com/jaemk/self_update
- patchify crate: https://github.com/danwilliams/patchify
- trust project: CI release builds
- ACFS manifest pattern for tool distribution

## Dependencies
- CI/CD Pipeline (beads_rust-na7)
- version Command (beads_rust-k8p) - completed","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"SwiftGrove","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T18:48:13.745544823Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T16:09:49.261619258Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T16:09:49.261619258Z","close_reason":"Duplicate bead (second- prefix) - original is beads_rust-7nh","external_ref":"second-7nh","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-lhk","title":"Unit tests: storage invariants (real SQLite)","description":"Add unit tests for SQLite storage invariants: schema shape, id prefix/short hash format, dedup/content-hash behavior, label/dep CRUD, ready/blocked queries, and sort/filter correctness.","acceptance_criteria":"1) Tests exercise SqliteStorage APIs against a real temp DB (no mocks).
2) Invariants validated: schema columns/indexes, deterministic IDs, dedup rules, ready/blocked computations, label/dep edges.
3) Tests are deterministic and pass under cargo test.","notes":"Added storage invariant tests in tests/storage_invariants.rs:
- schema tables/columns present
- label CRUD roundtrip
- dependency CRUD + blocked cache contents
- ready filters exclude blocked/deferred + label AND
- list filters (title/prio/include_closed + limit)
- content_hash lookup via upsert_issue_for_import
Also added #![allow(dead_code)] in tests/common helpers to avoid warnings when compiling integration tests.
Ran: cargo test --test storage_invariants (pass).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T16:17:52.471247185Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T16:42:24.265879571Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T16:42:23.215070820Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}

-{"id":"beads_rust-lr74","title":"[EPIC] agentic_coding_flywheel_setup #68: Add Root VPS AGENTS.md with Flywheel Commands","description":"## GitHub Issue Reference
- **Repo:** Dicklesworthstone/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup
- **Issue:** #68
- **Reporter:** maksymsherman
- **Created:** 2026-01-24

## Problem Statement
Flywheel commands and information are scattered across multiple webpages:
- https://agent-flywheel.com/wizard/launch-onboarding
- https://agent-flywheel.com/workflow
- https://agent-flywheel.com/learn (and lessons on VPS)
- https://agent-flywheel.com/learn/flywheel-loop

The user proposes creating an AGENTS.md file in the ROOT of the VPS (not in a repo) with a consolidated description of all available tools, updated weekly.

## Current State
- agentic_coding_flywheel_setup repo HAS AGENTS.md (33KB, updated Jan 21)
- But this is in the repo, not at VPS root
- No /AGENTS.md exists at VPS filesystem root

## User's Vision
1. Each VPS set up using the script gets /AGENTS.md
2. Contains detailed description of ALL available flywheel tools
3. Updated as part of weekly VPS update cycle

## Implementation Considerations

### What to Include in Root AGENTS.md
- All flywheel CLI tools (ru, br, bv, ntm, etc.)
- Common workflows and commands
- Links to detailed documentation
- Quick reference for frequently used operations

### Generation Strategy Options
1. **Static file:** Create once, manually update
2. **Generated file:** Script that aggregates tool --help outputs
3. **Templated file:** Markdown template with version stamps

### Update Mechanism
- Part of weekly VPS update script
- Could regenerate from tool versions
- Or pull from central repository

## Success Criteria
- /AGENTS.md exists on VPS root after setup
- Contains consolidated flywheel tool documentation
- Has update mechanism for weekly refresh
- Agents can find it easily (standard location)

## Overarching Goals
This serves the flywheel ecosystem by:
- Reducing cognitive load for new users
- Providing single source of truth on each VPS
- Making tools discoverable for agents","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-25T04:03:24.112845679Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T04:10:15.320818730Z","external_ref":"github:Dicklesworthstone/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup#68","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli"],"comments":[{"id":349,"issue_id":"beads_rust-lr74","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Background: The Flywheel Documentation Problem

The agentic coding flywheel is powerful but complex. Knowledge is scattered across:
- agent-flywheel.com (multiple pages)
- Individual tool READMEs
- Skill files in ~/.claude/skills/
- Lesson content on VPS

New users struggle to find the right command. Even experienced users forget less-common operations.

## User Pain Point

The reporter (maksymsherman) articulates this well: "It is sometimes challenging to remember all the possible flywheel commands." This is a discoverability problem.

## Solution: System-Level AGENTS.md

Unlike per-repo AGENTS.md files, this is a VPS-level file at /AGENTS.md:
- Always available regardless of current directory
- Covers ALL flywheel tools, not just one project
- Updated automatically as tools evolve

## Why Generate (Not Static)?

Static documentation becomes stale. By generating from tool --help outputs:
- Version info stays current
- New commands appear automatically
- Deprecated commands disappear
- No manual sync needed

## Integration with Weekly Updates

Flywheel VPS instances already have update mechanisms. Adding AGENTS.md regeneration:
- Minimal additional complexity
- Ensures documentation freshness
- Follows existing patterns

## Success Vision

A new flywheel user should be able to:
1. SSH into VPS
2. `cat /AGENTS.md`
3. Find the command they need
4. Get started immediately

No hunting through websites or asking for help.","created_at":"2026-01-25T04:10:15Z"}]}

No hunting through websites or asking for help.","created_at":"2026-01-25T04:10:15Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-lr74.1","title":"Design root AGENTS.md structure and content plan","description":"## Context
Part of agentic_coding_flywheel_setup #68 fix. Design the structure for the root /AGENTS.md.

## Content Inventory

### Core Flywheel Tools
Gather documentation for each tool:

1. **ru (Repo Utility)**
   - Multi-repo management
   - Sync, status, review commands

2. **br (Beads Rust)**
   - Issue tracking
   - Create, list, show, close, dep commands

3. **bv (Beads Viewer)**
   - TUI for beads
   - Graph visualization

4. **ntm (Named Tmux Manager)**
   - Agent session orchestration
   - Robot API, health, bulk-assign

5. **Agent CLI Tools**
   - cc (Claude Code)
   - cod (Codex)
   - gmi (Gemini)

### Workflow Documentation
- Flywheel loop overview
- Multi-agent orchestration
- Daily/weekly routines

### Quick Reference Sections
- Most common commands (cheat sheet)
- Troubleshooting common issues
- Where to get help

## Document Structure Proposal
```markdown
# Flywheel VPS - Agent Guidelines

## Quick Start
[5-10 most essential commands]

## Tools Reference
### ru - Multi-Repo Management
### br - Issue Tracking
### bv - Beads Visualization
### ntm - Agent Orchestration
### Agent CLIs

## Workflows
### The Flywheel Loop
### Multi-Agent Sessions
### Daily Maintenance

## Troubleshooting

## Resources
[Links to detailed docs]
```

## Deliverable
- Finalized document structure
- List of all tools to document
- Content outline for each section","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T04:03:53.947738340Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T09:50:17.175942200Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T09:50:17.175923565Z","close_reason":"Plan documented in agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/docs/ROOT_AGENTS_MD_PLAN.md","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-lr74.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-lr74","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T04:07:21Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":350,"issue_id":"beads_rust-lr74.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Plan drafted in /data/projects/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/docs/ROOT_AGENTS_MD_PLAN.md (structure + tool list + section outlines).","created_at":"2026-01-25T09:50:11Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-lr74.2","title":"Create AGENTS.md generation script for VPS root","description":"## Context
Part of agentic_coding_flywheel_setup #68 fix. Create a script that generates /AGENTS.md.

## Why a Script (Not Static File)
- Tools evolve, documentation should stay current
- Can pull version info dynamically
- Enables weekly automated updates
- Single source of truth for generation logic

## Script Design

### Location
- agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/scripts/generate-root-agents-md.sh
- Or: /usr/local/bin/flywheel-update-agents-md

### Script Logic
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# Generate /AGENTS.md for flywheel VPS

OUTPUT="/AGENTS.md"
TIMESTAMP=$(date -Iseconds)

cat > "$OUTPUT" << 'HEADER'
# Flywheel VPS - Agent Guidelines
> Auto-generated: $TIMESTAMP
> Regenerate: flywheel-update-agents-md

HEADER

# Add tool documentation
for tool in ru br bv ntm; do
  echo "## $tool"
  $tool --help 2>/dev/null | head -50
  echo ""
done

# Add static workflow content from template
cat /path/to/workflows-template.md >> "$OUTPUT"
```

### Template Content
Static sections that don't change often:
- Flywheel loop explanation
- Common workflows
- Troubleshooting tips
- Resource links

### Permissions
- Script runs as root or with sudo
- Output file readable by all users
- /AGENTS.md owned by root:root, mode 644

## Testing
1. Run script manually
2. Verify /AGENTS.md created
3. Verify content is accurate
4. Test with different tool versions

## Deliverable
- Working generation script
- Template for static content
- Installation instructions","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"TopazBadger","created_at":"2026-01-25T04:04:35.670968653Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T09:50:23.108090483Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-lr74.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-lr74","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T04:07:21Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-lr74.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-lr74.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T04:07:21Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-lr74.3","title":"Integrate AGENTS.md generation into VPS setup and update cycle","description":"## Context
Part of agentic_coding_flywheel_setup #68 fix. Integrate the generation script into VPS lifecycle.

## Integration Points

### 1. Initial VPS Setup
Modify the main setup script to:
- Install the generation script
- Run it once to create initial /AGENTS.md
- Add to PATH if needed

Location in setup flow:
- After all tools are installed
- Before final "setup complete" message

### 2. Weekly Update Cycle
The user mentions "weekly VPS update cycle". Integrate by:

Option A: Cron job
```bash
# /etc/cron.weekly/flywheel-agents-md
#!/bin/bash
/usr/local/bin/flywheel-update-agents-md
```

Option B: Part of existing update script
If there's already a weekly update script, add a call to the generator.

Option C: systemd timer
```ini
[Unit]
Description=Update Flywheel AGENTS.md

[Timer]
OnCalendar=weekly
Persistent=true

[Install]
WantedBy=timers.target
```

### 3. Manual Trigger
Users should be able to manually regenerate:
```bash
sudo flywheel-update-agents-md
```

## Files to Modify
- agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/setup.sh (or main script)
- Add cron job or systemd timer
- Update documentation

## Verification
1. Fresh VPS setup creates /AGENTS.md
2. Weekly update regenerates /AGENTS.md
3. Manual command works
4. No errors in logs

## Rollback Plan
If issues arise:
- Script is idempotent (can rerun safely)
- Old /AGENTS.md is overwritten (consider backup?)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T04:05:17.433507169Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T04:06:36.618201521Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-lr74.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-lr74","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T04:07:21Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-lr74.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-lr74.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T04:07:21Z","created_by":"import"}]}
